You should treat Eclipse as a damage cooldown and apply the standard rules you would on any other damage cooldown. This means to press it with as much resource available as possible while not overcapping the cooldown, and trying to pair it together with your other cooldowns.
Entering any Eclipse or pressing Celestial Alignment/Incarnation resets your current [spell=343647] to 6 seconds no matter what your previous duration was.

Members of the Dreamgrove discord [url=https://www.dreamgrove.gg/planner]developed this cooldown planner tool[/url] including the many CDR options in the Balance Druid tree. This tool can be pretty handy if you are interested in seeing how they interact with each other or want to plan cooldowns for an upcoming boss.
You can hold a charge of [spell=194223] at any time to better line up with a mechanic as long as you are not sitting on 2 charges.

Remember that [spell=434249] lets you hold any use of [spell=205636], [spell=194223]/[spell=102560] and [spell=391528] for up to 15 seconds and will reduce the cooldown depending on how long it was held.

On low target count fights like Vaelgor & Ezzorak, Lightblinded Vanguard or various two-target fights from the M+ dungeon pool, it is now worth keeping track of [spell=450212] on your targets when playing [spell=393940]. Stellar Amplification is a debuff that gets applied from [spell=78674] and increases the damage the target takes from a lot of your damage sources. Playing around this effect can be annoying since it requires you to constantly swap your target between spending free Starsurge procs to keep this up on both targets.
Whenever playing Starweaver you also want to bank up procs before entering your [spell=1239669] in order to spend [spell=1261564] as soon as possible inside your [spell=394048] window.

rotationST.name: Trinket Usage We usually want to pair stat-granting on-use trinkets with [spell=288613]. [item=193701] should be used shortly before [spell=288613]; avoid spending time casting it after Trueshot has already been popped. Ideally, pair it with [spell=257044] as in the opener.
Spend the [spell=260240] stacks generated by [spell=19434] and [spell=257044] by using [spell=257620]. You should also use it to activate [spell=257621] if it is down, regardless of other conditions.
Cast [spell=257044] if [spell=389019] is either down, or would expire before your next [spell=19434], or regardless, if your target is below 20% HP.

Activate [spell=288613], your major cooldown. We ideally press Trueshot right before we normally intend to [spell=19434], and ideally, right or shortly after a [spell=257044], so that you carry a fresh [spell=389019] into your cooldowns. It is extremely important that you never cast [spell=288613] and [spell=260243] back-to-back in any order, to avoid wasting [spell=473370]. Always fire, ideally, an [spell=19434] between them.
Cast [spell=260243] on cooldown. Never cast Volley directly before or after [spell=288613], as that wastes [spell=473370].

With [spell=473520] talented, cast [spell=212431] outside of [spell=288613] and when you do not already have a [spell=194595] proc, to give yourself one, if [spell=19434] has 1 or fewer charges available.
Cast [spell=1264902] when you have no [spell=19434]s remaining, or if [spell=288613] is about to run out.
Cast [spell=56641] as a filler. Do not overlook the importance of filling every "empty" moment with these, as they generate significant Focus and [spell=19434] cooldown reduction.

rotationST.name: With [spell=1263249] at level 90, [spell=205021] will have 2 charges, which provides significant flexibility in when you do deal damage. Keep it on cooldown, but as long as you have less than two charges you have a lot of choice in when you use the other charge. In particular, this has the potential to be very strong when bosses have vulnerability phases or spawn priority adds that must be killed.

[spell=1244110] grants two charges of both [spell=11426] and [spell=45438]/[spell=414659]. This makes it much easier to ensure that you have defensive cooldowns available for big damage events and offers a huge optimization opportunity if you're interesting in carefully scripting out all of your defensive usages in a boss fight.
